name: Gaussian Per-Token Loss Reweighting: A Negative Result
author: Julian Tang
base_submission: PR #180 by thwu1 — 10L Int5-MLP + BigramHash(10240) + SWA(0.4) + WD=0.04
blurb: Tests whether Gaussian per-token loss reweighting (focus training on tokens whose loss is near the batch mean) helps in the 16MB/10min setting. Single-hyperparameter addition (TOKEN_WEIGHT_SIGMA=2.0) to PR #180. Training-time weighted val_loss looks much better (1.0182 vs 1.1542 at step 6500), but final unweighted sliding-window BPB regresses by +0.0143 (1.1585 vs 1.1442). The weighted-loss improvement is an artifact of optimizing a different objective than the metric measures — a concrete cautionary tale for anyone tempted by per-token loss shaping.
